%% echo: false
---
title: "From Computer Vision Student to Machine Learning Engineer"
subtitle: "A staged teaching arc from research notebook to production ML workflow"
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: false
execute:
  eval: false
  echo: true
---

This module follows one small semantic segmentation project as it moves from a research-style notebook to a workflow another researcher or engineer can trust, rerun, and change.

The goal is not model accuracy. The goal is to make the transition from data science experimentation to production ML practice concrete.

## Presentation Stages

| Stage | Focus | Link |
|---|---|---|
| 0 | Research-style Pascal VOC training setup in a notebook | [Research Experiment](nbs/00_research_experiment.html) |
| 1 | A collaborator joins; code becomes configurable and `train.py` becomes agnostic | [Collaboration And Configuration](nbs/01_collaboration_config_runner.html) |
| 2 | MLflow tracking records params, metrics, checkpoints, and optional registry hooks | [MLflow Tracking And Registry](nbs/02_mlflow_tracking_registry.html) |
| 3 | Tests and CI protect the workflow contract without running heavy training | [CI Quality Gate](nbs/03_ci_quality_gate.html) |
| Advanced | Actual MLflow training run with artifacts, model logging, and optional registration | [Advanced MLflow Training](nbs/04_advanced_mlflow_training.html) |

## Project Arc

```{mermaid}
%%| eval: true
%%| echo: false
flowchart LR
  A[Research experiment] --> B[Collaborator changes config]
  B --> C[Agnostic train.py]
  C --> D[MLflow run history]
  D --> E[Tests and CI]
```

## Demo Commands

Install dependencies:

```bash
uv sync --dev
```

Run fast tests:

```bash
uv run pytest
```

Run a tiny real VOC training job:

```bash
uv run python train.py --config params.yaml
```

Run a fast synthetic smoke check:

```bash
uv run python train.py --config params.yaml --synthetic --model tiny --no-mlflow
```

Run a fast MLflow smoke check with model logging:

```bash
uv run python train.py --config params.yaml --synthetic --model tiny --max-samples 4 --max-batches 1 --log-model
```
